page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-32xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The system is equipped with 1024.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. The processor architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

In benchmark testing, the r7iz.metal-32xl demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance. Multi-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the top 10% of tested servers, supported by a 60 MB L3 cache. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with read-only small block cached transfers reaching 11388.44 GB/sec and large block uncached reads at 480.81 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier, with Redis achieving 155.31 million operations per second.

This server does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. It provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps to support high-throughput network storage and clustering. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included. The bare-metal configuration eliminates hypervisor overhead, making it highly efficient for resource-dense workloads, though the lack of integrated GPUs limits its utility for native hardware-accelerated deep learning.

Given its massive DDR5 memory capacity, high CPU clock speed, and top-tier database benchmark results, the r7iz.metal-32xl is optimized for memory-intensive databases, high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale software compilation, and demanding enterprise applications.
description: The Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-32xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs (64 physical cores) powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processors running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and integrated GPUs. Network connectivity is supported by a 50 Gbps baseline bandwidth. In benchmark evaluations, the server delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations, with Redis SET operations reaching 155.31 million operations per second. Its hardware profile is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, making it an excellent fit for large-scale databases, high-performance computing, and enterprise applications requiring high clock speeds and massive memory capacity without hypervisor overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-32xl is a bare-metal server with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 1024 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.

tagline: A high-frequency bare-metal server combining massive DDR5 memory capacity with top-tier multi-core processing power for demanding enterprise databases.

1024.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage included
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, enterprise applications, and HPC workloads
